task_completion.description: Agent logged into the Splunk web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Splunk web interface (localhost:8000) logged in as 'admin'.

task_completion.description: Created workflow action 'AbuseIPDB_Check' with the correct label and sourcetype.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk 'Workflow actions' settings page shows an action named 'AbuseIPDB_Check' with the label 'Check IP on AbuseIPDB' applied to the sourcetype 'linux_secure'.

task_completion.description: Configured 'AbuseIPDB_Check' with the correct URL and window settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The configuration for 'AbuseIPDB_Check' shows the action type as 'link', the URI as 'https://www.abuseipdb.com/check/$src_ip$', and the target as a new window ('_blank').

task_completion.description: Created workflow action 'Deep_IP_Investigation' with the correct label.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk 'Workflow actions' settings page shows an action named 'Deep_IP_Investigation' with the label 'Investigate this IP across all indexes'.

task_completion.description: Configured 'Deep_IP_Investigation' as a search action across multiple indexes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The configuration for 'Deep_IP_Investigation' shows the action type as 'search', the search string includes an IP token and queries multiple indexes (e.g., 'index=*'), and it is set to open in a new window.

task_completion.description: Created saved search 'Top_Attacking_IPs' querying 'security_logs' for failed authentications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk 'Reports' or 'Searches' page shows a saved search named 'Top_Attacking_IPs'. The search string must include 'index=security_logs', logic to find failed authentications, an aggregation by source IP (e.g., 'stats count by src_ip'), and a descending sort (e.g., 'sort - count').

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk web interface to create the objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the Splunk web UI (Settings > Fields > Workflow actions, and Search & Reporting) rather than using terminal commands (like curl) to interact with the Splunk REST API.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Splunk configuration forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent filling out the Splunk web forms for Workflow Actions and Save As Report/Search dialogs, rather than just editing text files in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing Splunk .conf files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent opening terminal editors (like nano or vim) to directly modify props.conf, savedsearches.conf, or workflow_actions.conf.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must use the Splunk web interface (localhost:8000) with credentials admin / SplunkAdmin1!. Three specific objects must be created: 1) A workflow action named exactly 'AbuseIPDB_Check' pointing to 'abuseipdb.com'. 2) A workflow action named exactly 'Deep_IP_Investigation'. 3) A saved search named exactly 'Top_Attacking_IPs' that queries the 'security_logs' index.
